one_time_description: Wildfire support expenditures of $4,780,463 with corresponding revenue recognized; non-recurring emergency program
economic_notes: Nation owns interests in Atikameg Construction and Oilfield Maintenance Corporation (100%), Atikameg Timber Limited Partnership (100%), Atikameg Timber Ltd. (100%), Kee Tas Kee Now Sawmill Limited Partnership (25%), Kee Tas Kee Now Sawmill Ltd. (33.3%), and Whitefish Lake First Nation #459 Child and Family Services (100%). Combined revenues of controlled entities exceeded $31.5M. Trust fund (Agriculture Benefit Fund) balance of ~$69.6M managed by CIBC Private Wealth Management. Distribution revenue of $1,200,000 received from Kee Tas Kee Now Sawmill Limited Partnership.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on two bases: (1) ARO liabilities under PS 3280 not identified, adjustments unknown; (2) investments in government business enterprises audited insufficiently — carrying amounts and equity in net income unverified. Six entities accounted for by modified equity method (not fully consolidated). Large trust fund portfolio (~$77.4M investments) derived from land claim and agricultural benefit settlements. Wildfire support was a non-recurring program ($4.78M revenue and expense). Excess of revenue over expenditures on Statement of Change in Net Financial Assets ($6,731,659) differs from income statement surplus ($3,218,409) due to trust fund and other adjustments — the schedule appears to include trust fund movements differently. Schedule 1 shows change in accumulated surplus of -$176,813 which reconciles opening $150,894,268 to closing $150,717,455. Total expenditures per Schedule 4 ($37,221,950) includes amortization ($3,338,372) and differs from program expenditures ($33,883,578) due to inclusion of amortization in Schedule 4.
